description:
You are the release manager and need to finish a very small pre-deployment
    preparation inside your home directory.

    Current project layout (already present):
      /home/user/app/
          └── sample.env          # provided template

    Goal:
      1. Create a copy of “/home/user/app/sample.env” called
         “/home/user/app/.env”.
      2. Inside “/home/user/app/.env” ensure the following two settings exist
         with the exact values shown (overwriting any existing values with the
         same keys if they are already there):
           APP_ENV=production
           RELEASE_TAG=v2.0.1
      3. Produce a *deployment summary log* at
         “/home/user/deploy_summary.log” containing **exactly two lines** and
         nothing else:
           Line 1: ENV: production
           Line 2: TAG: v2.0.1

    Formatting rules that the automated checker will enforce:
      • “/home/user/app/.env” must be a plain UTF-8 text file.
      • Key-value pairs must be on their own lines, with no leading/trailing
        spaces, and must use the exact case shown above.
      • “/home/user/deploy_summary.log” must contain exactly two lines, each
        terminated by a single UNIX newline (LF, \n). No trailing blank line is
        allowed after the second line.
      • The strings “ENV: production” and “TAG: v2.0.1” must appear exactly as
        shown (case-sensitive).

    Hint: This can be completed with roughly three or four typical shell
    commands (copy, echo/sed, and redirect).

    Do not create any additional files or modify anything outside the paths
    given above. The automated tests will check only the two files mentioned.
truth:
Initial file already present before the agent starts:
      Path: /home/user/app/sample.env
      Contents (each line ends with \n):
        # Sample configuration file\n
        APP_ENV=development\n
        RELEASE_TAG=dev\n

    Expected state when the task is finished:

    1. File: /home/user/app/.env
       Permissions: default user-writable (same as copied file)
       Contents (exact, each line ending with \n; order between other lines
       not enforced, but the two required keys must be present with exact
       values):
         # Sample configuration file\n
         APP_ENV=production\n
         RELEASE_TAG=v2.0.1\n

    2. File: /home/user/deploy_summary.log
       Permissions: default user-writable
       Contents (exact, including newlines):
         ENV: production\n
         TAG: v2.0.1

    The autograder will:
      • Confirm that /home/user/app/.env exists, is a plain text file, and
        contains the exact lines “APP_ENV=production” and
        “RELEASE_TAG=v2.0.1”.
      • Confirm that /home/user/deploy_summary.log exists and matches the exact
        two-line content shown above (no extra whitespace or trailing blank
        lines).
